The codebase is at version 2.175 (commit: 1f949804). ## Issue to Resolve Improve "discard changes" dialog options If you click Add note and type something and then close the window without saving the changes, the following message pops up. This pop up also appears at other places (for e.g. when leaving the Deck Options page without clicking Save). The issue with this pop up is that "OK" and "Cancel" don't seem to be the correct responses to a message that says "Close and lose current input". What about using the following instead? - Anki Desktop: - Google Photos: ## Additional Context from Issue Discussion Will use `TR` from backend Good first issue, I have fixed it if anyone wants to drop a PR here's the patch ``` Subject: [PATCH] string from backend --- Index: AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt IDEA additional info: Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP <+>UTF-8 =================================================================== diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt --- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt (revision 906dd02571179f3096af4cdf32a924385c2af4b2) +++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/EditDeckDescriptionDialog.kt (date 1708346927147) @@ -24,6 +24,7 @@ import androidx.fragment.app.DialogFragment import com.google.android.material.appbar.MaterialToolbar import com.google.android.material.textfield.TextInputEditText +import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager import com.ichi2.anki.CollectionManager.withCol import com.ichi2.anki.R import com.ichi2.anki.StudyOptionsFragment @@ -106,7 +107,7 @@ } Timber.i("asking if user should discard changes") - DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(requireContext()) { + DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(requireContext(),getString(R.string.discard), CollectionManager.TR.addingKeepEditing(), CollectionManager.TR.addingDiscardCurrentInput()) { closeWithoutSaving() } } Index: AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt IDEA additional info: Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP <+>UTF-8 =================================================================== diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt --- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt (revision 906dd02571179f3096af4cdf32a924385c2af4b2) +++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplateBrowserAppearanceEditor.kt (date 1708345145742) @@ -98,7 +98,9 @@ } private fun showDiscardChangesDialog() { - DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(this, ::discardChangesAndClose) + DiscardChangesDialog.showDialog(this,getString(R.string.discard), CollectionManager.TR.addingKeepEditing(), CollectionManager.TR.addingDiscardCurrentInput()){ + discardChangesAndClose() + } } private fun showRestoreDefaultDialog() { Index: AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/DiscardChangesDialog.kt IDEA additional info: Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP <+>UTF-8 =================================================================== diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/DiscardChangesDialog.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/DiscardChangesDialog.kt --- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/DiscardChangesDialog.kt (revision 906dd02571179f3096af4cdf32a924385c2af4b2) +++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/dialogs/DiscardChangesDialog.kt (date 1708344904076) @@ -23,15 +23,16 @@ import com.ichi2.utils.positiveButton import com.ichi2.utils.show -class DiscardChangesDialog { - assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", getAlertDialogText(true), getResourceString(R.string.discard_unsaved_changes)) + assertEquals("Wrong dialog shown?", getAlertDialogText(true), "Discard current input?") clickAlertDialogButton(DialogInterface.BUTTON_POSITIVE, false) advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep() assertFalse("model change not cleared despite discarding changes?", testEditor.modelHasChanged()) Index: AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/DeckOptions.kt IDEA additional info: Subsystem: com.intellij.openapi.diff.impl.patch.CharsetEP <+>UTF-8 =================================================================== diff --git a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/DeckOptions.kt b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/DeckOptions.kt --- a/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/DeckOptions.kt (revision 906dd02571179f3096af4cdf32a924385c2af4b2) +++ b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/pages/DeckOptions.kt (date 1708345145717) @@ -55,7 +55,7 @@ private val onBackSaveCallback = object : OnBackPressedCallback(true) { override fun handleOnBackPressed() { Timber.v("DeckOptions: showing 'discard changes'") -  39→object CollectionManager {
 40→    /**
 41→     * The currently active backend, which is created on demand via [ensureBackend], and
 42→     * implicitly via [ensureOpen] and routines like [withCol].
 43→     * The backend is long-lived, and will generally only be closed when switching interface
 44→     * languages or changing schema versions. A closed backend cannot be reused, and a new one
 45→     * must be created.
 46→     */
 47→    private var backend: Backend? = null
 48→
 49→    /**
 50→     * The current collection, which is opened on demand via [withCol]. If you need to
 51→     * close and reopen the collection in an atomic operation, add a new method that
 52→     * calls [withQueue], and then executes [ensureClosedInner] and [ensureOpenInner] inside it.
 53→     * A closed collection can be detected via [withOpenColOrNull] or by checking [Collection.dbClosed].
 54→     */
 55→    private var collection: Collection? = null
 56→
 57→    private var queue: CoroutineDispatcher = Dispatchers.IO.limitedParallelism(1)
 58→
 59→    @VisibleForTesting
 60→    var emulateOpenFailure = false
 61→
 62→    /** A speed optimisation to remove the logging function of the collection */
 63→    @VisibleForTesting
 64→    var disableLogFile: Boolean = false
 65→
 66→    /**
 67→     * Execute the provided block on a serial background queue, to ensure
 68→     * concurrent access does not happen.
 69→     *
 70→     * The background queue is run in a [Dispatchers.IO] context.
 71→     * It's important that the block is not suspendable - if it were, it would allow
 72→     * multiple requests to be interleaved when a suspend point was hit.
 73→     *
 74→     * TODO Disallow running functions that are supposed to be run inside the queue outside of it.
 75→     *   For instance, this can be done by marking a [block] with a context
 76→     *   that cannot be instantiated outside of this class:
 77→     *
 78→     *       suspend fun<T> withQueue(block: context(Queue) () -> T): T {
 79→     *          return withContext(collectionOperationsDispatcher) {
 80→     *              block(queue)
 81→     *          }
 82→     *      }
 83→     *
 84→     *   Then, only functions that are also marked can be run inside the block:
 85→     *
 86→     *       context(Queue) suspend fun canOnlyBeRunInWithQueue()
 87→     */
 88→    private suspend fun<T> withQueue(@WorkerThread block: CollectionManager.() -> T): T {
 89→        return withContext(queue) {
 90→            this@CollectionManager.block()
 91→        }
 92→    }
 93→
 94→    /**
 95→     * Execute the provided block with the collection, opening if necessary.
 96→     *
 97→     * Calls are serialized, and run in background [Dispatchers.IO] thread.
 98→     *
 99→     * Parallel calls to this function are guaranteed to be serialized, so you can be
100→     * sure the collection won't be closed or modified by another thread. This guarantee
101→     * does not hold if legacy code calls [getColUnsafe].
102→     */
103→    suspend fun <T> withCol(@WorkerThread block: Collection.() -> T): T {
104→        return withQueue {
105→            ensureOpenInner()
106→            block(collection!!)
107→        }
108→    }
109→
110→    /**
111→     * Execute the provided block if the collection is already open. See [withCol] for more.
112→     * Since the block may return a null value, and a null value will also be returned in the
113→     * case of the collection being closed, if the calling code needs to distinguish between
114→     * these two cases, it should wrap the return value of the block in a class (eg Optional),
115→     * instead of returning a nullable object.
116→     */
117→    suspend fun<T> withOpenColOrNull(@WorkerThread block: Collection.() -> T): T? {
118→        return withQueue {
119→            if (collection != null && !collection!!.dbClosed) {
120→                block(collection!!)
121→            } else {
122→                null
123→            }
124→        }
125→    }
126→
127→    /**
128→     * Return a handle to the backend, creating if necessary. This should only be used
129→     * for routines that don't depend on an open or closed collection, such as checking
130→     * the current progress state when importing a colpkg file. While the backend is
131→     * thread safe and can be accessed concurrently, if another thread closes the collection
132→     * and you call a routine that expects an open collection, it will result in an error.
133→     */
134→    fun getBackend(): Backend {
135→        if (backend == null) {
136→            runBlocking { withQueue { ensureBackendInner() } }
137→        }
138→        return backend!!
139→    }
140→
141→    /**
142→     * Translations provided by the Rust backend/Anki desktop code.
143→     */
144→    val TR: Translations
145→        get() {
146→            // we bypass the lock here so that translations are fast - conflicts are unlikely,
147→            // as the backend is only ever changed on language preference change or schema switch
148→            return getBackend().tr
149→        }
150→
151→    fun compareAnswer(expected: String, given: String): String {
152→        // bypass the lock, as the type answer code is heavily nested in non-suspend functions
153→        return getBackend().compareAnswer(expected, given)
154→    }
155→
156→    /**
157→     * Close the currently cached backend and discard it. Useful when enabling the V16 scheduler in the
158→     * dev preferences, or if the active language changes. Saves and closes the collection if open.
159→     */
160→    suspend fun discardBackend() {
161→        withQueue {
162→            discardBackendInner()
163→        }
164→    }
165→
166→    /** See [discardBackend]. This must only be run inside the queue. */
167→    private fun discardBackendInner() {
168→        ensureClosedInner()
169→        if (backend != null) {
170→            backend!!.close()
171→            backend = null
172→        }
173→    }
174→
175→    /**
176→     * Open the backend if it's not already open.
177→     */
178→    private suspend fun ensureBackend() {
179→        withQueue {
180→            ensureBackendInner()
181→        }
182→    }
183→
184→    /** See [ensureBackend]. This must only be run inside the queue. */
185→    private fun ensureBackendInner() {
186→        if (backend == null) {
187→            backend = BackendFactory.getBackend()
188→        }
189→    }
190→
191→    /**
192→     * If the collection is open, close it.
193→     */
194→    suspend fun ensureClosed() {
195→        withQueue {
196→            ensureClosedInner()
197→        }
198→    }
